_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Statewide-exec batch: FL governor field (Jolly, Pizzo, Collins, Renner) + DE LG (Kyle Evans Gay, roster-corrected from phantom tonya-williams). Evidence-first, every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

set.office: Lieutenant Governor (sitting · 2026 R candidate for Governor)

profile.background: Former FL State Senator SD-14 (Nov 2022-Aug 2025); Green Beret combat veteran; 21st Lieutenant Governor of Florida (appointed by DeSantis, sworn 2025-08-12). Running for Governor 2026.

jay-collins.notes_set: Evidence (state tier). Pro-life: voted YES on SB 300 (2023 six-week Heartbeat ban) and pledged money to defeat 2024 Amendment 4, calling it 'unequivocally wrong.' School choice: in the GOP Senate majority that passed HB 1 (2023) universal vouchers on party lines. Pro-police: Green Beret with public law-enforcement-support posture. Border: party-line passage of SB 1718 (2023) mandatory E-Verify. 2A: was the SENATE SPONSOR of SB 150 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry). No contrary (FALSE) evidence found; remaining cells null for lack of an individual record.

0.note: YES on SB 300 six-week ban; staunch public pro-life, opposed Amendment 4 as 'unequivocally wrong.'

4.note: Funded the anti-Amendment-4 (pro-life) side; no abortion-industry PAC funding on record.

0.note: In the GOP Senate majority that passed HB 1 (2023) universal school-choice vouchers on party lines.
Green Beret; public law-enforcement-support posture (affirmative pro-police advocacy).

3.note: Party-line passage of SB 1718 (2023) mandatory E-Verify for 25+-employee employers.

0.note: Senate sponsor of SB 150 (2023) permitless/constitutional carry.

set.office: Candidate for Governor of Florida (former FL House Speaker, 2022-2024)

profile.background: Former Speaker of the Florida House of Representatives (103rd, Nov 2022-Nov 2024), term-limited; 2026 R candidate for Governor.

paul-renner.notes_set: Evidence (state tier) — deep record as House Speaker 2022-24. Drove the 2023 six-week Heartbeat Protection Act (SBA Pro-Life thanked him by name). Under his speakership: trans-care ban for minors (SB 254) + bathroom bill (HB 1521); Parental Rights in Education expansion. HB 1 universal school choice was his 'top priority.' HB 3 (2024) porn age-verification + minor social-media limits was his 'key priority.' CBDC ban (SB 7054) and anti-ESG HB 3 (2023) passed his House. Death-penalty expansion (8-4 jury, child-rape death penalty); pledges faster executions. SB 1718 mandatory E-Verify + anti-sanctuary and SB 264 foreign-land-ownership ban under his House. Personally championed HB 543 permitless carry. DEVIATION (noted, not scored as a cell): as Speaker he blocked OPEN carry ('dead on arrival'), drawing GOA/RLC criticism, and did not pursue repeal of FL's 2018 red-flag law.

0.note: As Speaker drove the 2023 six-week Heartbeat Protection Act; SBA Pro-Life thanked him by name.

4.note: Pro-life legislative champion; no abortion-industry funding on record.

2.note: Under his speakership: trans-care ban for minors (SB 254) + bathroom bill (HB 1521) affirming biological sex.

4.note: Parental Rights in Education expansion / drag-ban under his House — opposes LGBTQ+ promotion in schools.

0.note: HB 1 (2023) universal school choice was his stated 'top priority.'

1.note: Parental Rights in Education expansion (parental notification on student well-being) passed his House.

2.note: Parental Rights in Education limits gender/SOGI instruction in K-12 under his House.

3.note: HB 3 (2024) porn age-verification + minor social-media limits was his 'key priority' ('a dark alley for our children').

4.note: Anti-ESG HB 3 (2023) was a priority of his speakership.

2.note: Death-penalty expansion (8-4 jury threshold, child-rape death penalty) under his House — proportional punishment of evildoers.
SB 1718 (2023) anti-sanctuary provisions passed his House.

3.note: SB 1718 (2023) mandatory E-Verify (25+ employees) passed his House.

4.note: SB 264 (2023) foreign/Chinese farmland-ownership ban passed his House.

0.note: Personally announced/championed HB 543 (2023) permitless carry: 'remove the government permission slip.'

profile.background: Former U.S. Rep FL-13 (2014-2017, as a Republican); registered Democrat in 2025; presumptive 2026 Democratic nominee for Governor of Florida (running mate Gwen Graham).

david-jolly.notes_set: Evidence (state tier) — CURRENT Democratic gubernatorial positions take priority over his 2014-17 GOP House record (which he has reversed). Abortion: now pro-choice, would codify Roe, says he 'wrongly conflated being pro-life with being anti-Roe.' Marriage: backed same-sex marriage since 2014. School choice: would cap voucher tuition and boost public-school funding. Immigration: opposes mandatory deportation, supports a documentation path, would strike down 287(g) ICE cooperation. Guns: wants an assault-weapons ban, universal background checks, licensing/registration, and to EXPAND red-flag laws. CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION (historical): his 2014-17 GOP record held several conservative positions (life-at-conception, secure-border-first, balanced-budget amendment, death penalty for cop-killers) that he has since abandoned as a Democrat.

0.note: Now pro-choice; says he 'wrongly conflated being pro-life with being anti-Roe' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pledges to codify the Roe-Casey framework and protect abortion as governor — opposite of abolition.

0.note: Publicly supports same-sex marriage (since 2014) — does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage in law.
Would cap voucher tuition inflation and dramatically increase public-school funding — constrains school choice.

1.note: Opposes mandatory deportation; supports a streamlined documentation path for the immigrant community.

2.note: Would strike down 287(g) ICE cooperation — pro-sanctuary posture.

0.note: Backs licensing and opposes loosening carry — against permitless carry.

1.note: Supports an assault-weapons ban, universal background checks, registration, and EXPANDING red-flag laws.

set.office: State Senator (sitting · 2026 NPA candidate for Governor)

profile.background: Sitting FL State Senator SD-37; left the Democratic Party 2025-04-24 to become NPA/independent (resigned as Senate Democratic Leader). Former Miami-Dade prosecutor. Running for Governor 2026 as an independent. De-duplicated 2026-06 — merged jason-pizzo gov-candidate record.

jason-wb-pizzo.notes_set: Evidence (state tier) — sitting senator's voting record. Progressive on life/marriage/family/2A but a notable CROSS-PARTY conservative deviation on law-and-order and immigration enforcement. Abortion: voted NO on SB 300 six-week ban; backed Amendment 4. Marriage/family: voted against Parental Rights in Education ('Don't Say Gay'); opposed HB 1 universal vouchers (ILA Education 0%). Public justice (DEVIATION+): former prosecutor, chaired Senate Criminal Justice, endorsed by law-enforcement unions (Broward PBA Legislator of the Year, FOP awards) — affirmative pro-police. Immigration (DEVIATION+): FILED a 2025 bill requiring ALL Florida employers to use E-Verify. 2A: voted NO on SB 150 permitless carry.

0.note: Voted NO on SB 300 six-week ban and supported pro-abortion Amendment 4 — does not affirm personhood.

1.note: Backed Amendment 4 abortion-rights measure — opposite of abolition.

2.note: Voted against Parental Rights in Education; pro-LGBTQ floor statement — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Opposed restrictions on LGBTQ instruction in schools.

0.note: Opposed HB 1 universal vouchers (ILA Education category 0%).

2.note: Voted against Parental Rights in Education — supports SOGI instruction in K-12.

0.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: former prosecutor, chaired Senate Criminal Justice, endorsed by law-enforcement unions (Broward PBA Legislator of the Year, FOP) — affirmative pro-police.

3.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: filed a 2025 bill requiring ALL Florida employers to use E-Verify.

0.note: Voted NO on SB 150 (2023) permitless carry.

profile.background: Delaware State Senator SD-5 (2021-2025); 27th Lieutenant Governor of Delaware (D), sworn 2025-01-21; term to 2029. Roster correction — replaces phantom record 'tonya-williams' (no such DE LG existed).

kyle-evans-gay.notes_set: Evidence (state tier) — roster-corrected record (the prior 'tonya-williams, DE LG' was a phantom; verified sitting LG is Kyle Evans Gay, D). Uniformly progressive; no conservative deviations found. Abortion: prime sponsor of multiple abortion-expansion bills and a constitutional amendment to enshrine abortion rights. Elections: chaired Senate Elections committee and passed SB 5 automatic voter registration at DMV. Public justice: sponsored HB 70 to abolish the death penalty. Guns: publicly supports permit-to-purchase and 'gun-violence prevention.' Marriage/family/economic/border: NO EVIDENCE found at cell level — left null.

0.note: Filed a constitutional amendment to enshrine the right to abortion; prime sponsor of abortion-expansion bills — does not affirm personhood.

1.note: Campaigns on 'the full spectrum of reproductive rights' — opposite of abolition.
Chaired Senate Elections cmte and passed SB 5 automatic voter registration at the DMV — opposite of citizenship-verification gatekeeping.

2.note: Sponsored HB 70 to abolish the death penalty — against proportional capital punishment of evildoers.

0.note: Supports a permit-to-purchase system — opposes permitless carry.

1.note: Supports permit-to-purchase and 'gun-violence prevention' gun-control measures.
